1	# Clean-room reimplementation protocol — tamarin-rs relicensing
2	
3	Purpose: independently recreate three modules of tamarin-rs (wellformedness
4	checker, graph/dot pipeline, web layer) without access to GPL-covered
5	expression, so the results are original works relicensable at will.
6	
7	## Rooms
8	- DIRTY ROOM (Kevin Milner + main-session assistant): has read the GPL Haskell
9	  source and the ported Rust modules. Produces only: interoperability headers
10	  (type surfaces, entry-point signatures, comments stripped), oracle wrappers,
11	  and behavioral bug reports. Never contributes implementation text.
12	- CLEAN ROOM (isolated agents, one per cluster): receive ONLY the materials
13	  listed per cluster below. Their complete tool-call transcripts are retained
14	  as evidence of non-access.
15	
16	## Clean-room materials (exhaustive)
17	1. This protocol, the cluster SPEC.md, and interface/ headers (type surfaces +
18	   boundary signatures only).
19	2. Black-box oracles: `wf_oracle.sh` (runs the compiled Haskell binary on an
20	   input file), `hs_server.sh` (serves a theory in the Haskell web UI for HTTP
21	   probing), pre-captured oracle outputs (oracle/captures/,
22	   oracle/captured_responses/), and GPL example .spthy files used solely as
23	   observation INPUTS.
24	3. Public Rust toolchain and general documentation.
25	
26	## Forbidden to the clean room
27	- Reading ANY file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/ — source, comments, git
28	  history — with the sole exceptions of executing the oracle scripts and
29	  reading oracle/captured_responses/ (captured program OUTPUT, not source).
30	- Fetching tamarin-prover source, docs describing its internals, or its GitHub.
31	- Reproducing any tamarin-prover implementation detail from model memory:
32	  every behavior must be derived from the oracle, the captures, or the SPEC.
33	  Exact output strings (warning texts, DOT syntax, HTML) MUST be taken from
34	  observed oracle output — that is compatibility content, not expression.
35	
36	## Audit & integration (after implementation)
37	1. Transcript audit: verify from the retained agent transcripts that no
38	   forbidden path was accessed.
39	2. Similarity audit: a reviewer (may see both sides) compares the clean code
40	   against the Haskell originals; flagged similarities are sent back as
41	   redo-instructions, never patched directly.
42	3. Behavioral acceptance: byte-parity corpus must pass.
43	4. Integration: dirty room may write thin adapters and mechanical renames to
44	   fit the workspace API, but must not transplant logic from the replaced
45	   modules. The replaced modules are deleted; their GPL-pending headers go
46	   with them (history retains them under GPL).

content.content: 1 # BEHAVIOR.md — observed spec of the tamarin web-UI constraint-system graph payload 2 3 All statements below are derived from OBSERVED oracle output: the 81 captured 4 crawl manifests under `oracle/captured_responses/` (14705 graph payloads) and 5 targeted live probing of the black-box HS binary (see QUERIES.log). No 6 tamarin-prover source was read. Where a behavior could not be pinned down 7 black-box it is marked **[GAP]**. 8 9 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 10 ## 1. Transport / payload family 11 12 A crawl manifest is JSON: `{base, lemmas, log, capped, manifest}` where 13 `manifest` maps a URL to `{kind, status, body}` (kind ∈ html|text|json|dot). 14 15 Graph-bearing URL families (theory index `N` replaces `#` in captures): 16 17 | URL family | kind | payload | 18 |---|---|---| 19 | `/thy/trace/N/interactive-graph-def/proof/[/...]` | `dot` | **the graph** — graphviz DOT text (`text/plain`) | 20 | `/thy/trace/N/intdot/proof/[/...]` | `html` | wrapper page embedding `` | 21 | `/thy/trace/N/main/cases/{raw,refined}/i/j` | `json` | `{html,title}`; html contains `` | 22 23 The DOT text at `interactive-graph-def` is the whole deliverable. `intdot` is a 24 fixed HTML shell (only the `` and the `dotsrc` path vary). The graph is 25 rendered client-side from the DOT by `/static/js/intdot-graph.es.js`. 26 27 **JSON graph payload** [GAP]: the binary exposes a *separate* `--with-json` 28 rendering backend (`tamarin-prover interactive --help`). No JSON rendering tool 29 is installed here and no `interactive-graph-def` body in the corpus is JSON — 30 the entire corpus is the DOT backend. JSON graph format is therefore unobserved. 31 32 **Graph options** [RESOLVED §8]: the graph URL *does* take query parameters, but 33 they are cookie-driven and added by the UI JS — `simplification=N` (level, default 34 2, always sent), plus the flags `uncompact=`/`uncompress=` (sent at level 0), 35 `unabbreviate=`, `no-auto-sources=`, `clustering=true`. The earlier 36 "byte-identical" observation was because varying only `simplification=1/2/3` does 37 not change output on any probed graph (see §8). The real transforms are triggered 38 by the boolean flags. See §8. 39 40 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 41 ## 2. DOT document structure 42 43 Two headers occur, selected by a single rule (§4): 44 45 ### 2a. Simple header (non-clustered) 46 ``` 47 digraph "G" { 48 nodesep="0.3"; 49 ranksep="0.3"; 50 node[fontsize="8",fontname="Helvetica",width="0.3",height="0.2"]; 51 edge[fontsize="8",fontname="Helvetica"]; 52 <statements…> 53 54 } 55 ``` 56 57 ### 2b. Compact header (clustered) 58 ``` 59 digraph "G" { 60 nodesep="0.8"; 61 ranksep="0.8"; 62 sep="4"; 63 splines="true"; 64 overlap="false"; 65 pack="true"; 66 packmode="cluster"; 67 concentrate="true"; 68 compound="true"; 69 remincross="true"; 70 mclimit="10"; 71 nslimit="20"; 72 nslimit1="20"; 73 ordering="out"; 74 rankdir="TB"; 75 showboxes="false"; 76 clusterrank="local"; 77 node[fontsize="8",fontname="Helvetica",width="0.3",height="0.2",margin="0.05,0.05",shape="ellipse"]; 78 edge[fontsize="8",fontname="Helvetica",penwidth="1.5",arrowsize="0.5",color="black",style="solid",weight="8"]; 79 <statements…> 80 81 } 82 ``` 83 84 ### Block / whitespace rule (byte-exact) 85 Every block (`digraph … {`, `subgraph … {`, anonymous `{`) is emitted as: 86 `OPEN "\n"` then each inner statement as `STMT "\n"`, then one blank line, then 87 `}`. i.e. the body always ends `…\n\n}`. The top-level digraph is followed by a 88 final `\n`. Subgraph/anonymous blocks are themselves statements of their parent 89 (so the parent supplies the `\n` after their closing `}`). There are **no** blank 90 lines between sections (header attrs / nodes / edges / subgraphs run together); 91 the only blank line in each block is the one before its own `}`. 92 An empty graph is header attrs + blank + `}` (154 bytes). 93 94 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 95 ## 3. Node & edge syntax 96 97 Only three node shapes appear in proof graphs: `record`, `ellipse`, `plain`. 98 99 ### 3a. Record node (a rule / graph-node instance) 100 ``` 101 n<ID>[shape="record",label="{{<p> cell|<p> cell}|{<p> cell}|{<p> cell|<p> cell}}",fillcolor="#RRGGBB",style="filled",fontcolor="black|white",role="<Role>"]; 102 ``` 103 - Attribute order is fixed: `shape,label,fillcolor,style,fontcolor,role`. 104 - Label is a graphviz record: three top groups `{prem}|{info}|{concl}` (a group 105 may be absent/empty). Each group = cells joined by `|`. Each cell = 106 `<portid> text` (port id like `n0`, a space, then the fact text). Ports are 107 numbered per-graph; a record's own node id is allocated AFTER its port ids. 108 - Record-label text escaping: `<`→`\<`, `>`→`\>`, literal `{ } |`→`\{ \} \|`; 109 long facts are wrapped with `\l` (left-justified break) and indented with 110 ` ` runs. (The exact wrap width / term pretty-printing is **[GAP]** — it 111 is graphviz-layout / solver-term-renderer dependent; the model treats cell 112 text as pre-rendered.) 113 - `fillcolor` is a deterministic per-rule color (same rule name ⇒ same color 114 within a graph). `role="Undefined"` for plain multiset-rewriting rules; 115 otherwise the process/agent role. `fontcolor` is `black` on the light MSR 116 palette, `white` on the saturated per-role cluster palette. Exact color hash 117 is **[GAP]**; the model carries colors explicitly. 118 119 ### 3b. Ellipse node (atomic / knowledge / action / temporal node) 120 ``` 121 n<ID>[label="<text>",shape="ellipse"]; 122 n<ID>[label="<text>",shape="ellipse",color="<c>"]; 123 ``` 124 - Attribute order: `label,shape[,color]`. Optional `color` (`gray` for `!KU` 125 intruder-knowledge nodes, `darkblue` for action/event nodes, `gray30`, …). 126 - Ellipse text is NOT record-escaped (`<`,`>` appear literally, e.g. 127 `Session( 'I', <pk(~ltkA), pkB>, … )`). 128 129 ### 3c. Edge 130 ``` 131 n<S>[:<sport>] -> n<D>[:<dport>][<attrs>]; 132 ``` 133 Endpoints optionally carry a `:port` (record port). `<attrs>` is a 134 comma-separated attribute list, in observed key order. Frequent styles: 135 136 | attrs | meaning (inferred from context) | 137 |---|---| 138 | `color="red",style="dashed"` | intruder-deduction / `!KU` edge | 139 | `style="bold",weight="10.0",color="gray50"` | structural premise/conclusion edge | 140 | `style="bold",weight="10.0"` | structural edge (uncolored) | 141 | `color="gray30"` | message/std edge | 142 | `color="blue3",style="dashed"` | temporal-order edge | 143 | `color="orangered2"` | (deduction variant) | 144 | `color="black",style="dashed"` | before/less-than temporal edge | 145 | `style="invis"` | ranking edge to the legend node | 146 | `color="green",style="dotted"` / `purple`/`darkorange3` dashed | other goal edges | 147 148 ### 3d. Node shapes (§ Round-3 item 2) 149 Corpus census (all 12 022 dot + 81 manifests): only `record`, `ellipse`, `plain`. 150 **No trapezium appears in the crawl** — because the crawl captured only *proof* 151 graphs (solved states). Live probing (NAXOS_eCK, NSLPK3 case-distinction graphs) 152 adds one more shape: 153 - **`invtrapezium`** — a `(#var, idx)` placeholder for an **unresolved graph node** 154 referenced by a still-open premise, e.g. `n9[label="(#i, 0)",shape="invtrapezium"]`. 155 `#var` is the (temporal) node variable, `idx` the premise index; the node is fed 156 by a structural edge from the conclusion that must satisfy that premise 157 (`n5:n2 -> n9[style="bold",weight="10.0",color="gray50"]`). Appears in both the 158 compressed default and the uncompressed variant, in every source-case graph 159 tested. Attr order = ellipse's (`label,shape`). 160 - **`trapezium`** (spec-named dual: an unresolved *source* node feeding a present 161 premise) was **NOT observed** in any probe (Session-3 NAXOS/NSLPK3 case graphs, 162 Session-5 re-scan of NSLPK3 `cases/{raw,refined}/i/j` for i≤12,j≤8) nor anywhere 163 in the corpus/manifests. The invtrapezium is the *conclusion→absent-node* case 164 (its edge points **into** it: `n5:n2 -> n6`), i.e. the "conclusion feeding a node 165 absent from the graph"; the un-seen dual would be the mirror (`absent -> premise`). 166 Recorded unobserved; `GraphNode::Shaped{label,shape,color}` (generic) can emit any 167 shape a caller supplies. 168 169 ### 3g. Bare-timepoint ellipse + the `#last` designated timepoint (§ Round-5 item 3) 170 An uncolored ellipse whose label is a bare timepoint variable `#<var>`: 171 `n<ID>[label="#<var>",shape="ellipse"];`. Corpus census of ellipse labels found, 172 besides the `!KU(..)@#t` / `#t:rule[..]` / `Action(..)@#t` forms, bare-timepoint 173 nodes `#i` (1328), `#decrypt` (790), `#t0…#t6`, `#j`, `#j.1`, and the **designated 174 last timepoint `#last`** (107, e.g. `24a119958f784d43.dot`). `#last` arises when a 175 constraint system carries a last timepoint (induction / trace-property proofs); it 176 is the target of a `color="black",style="dashed"` before-edge. All render as the 177 plain uncolored ellipse above. Modelled as `GraphNode::Temporal{var}` (with 178 `GraphNode::last()` for `#last`). 179 180 ### 3e. Node-id / port allocation (§ Round-3 item 3) — byte-verified over 12 022 181 One global monotonic counter, ids handed out in **emission order** so file order == 182 id order. For each node in order: 183 - a **record** takes one id per cell (in cell order: premises, then the info cell, 184 then conclusions) and **then** one id for the node itself — so a `k`-cell record 185 occupies `n<p>…n<p+k-1>` (ports) and `n<p+k>` (node); 186 - every other kind (ellipse / plain / invtrapezium) takes exactly one id. 187 188 Example: a 4-prem/1-info/3-concl record takes ports `n0…n6`, node `n7`; the next 189 ellipse is `n8`. Verified 12022/12022 in `mine_ids.py` and again in Rust 190 (`tests/alloc_corpus.rs`, via `NodeIdAllocator`). 191 192 ### 3f. Record-cell term rendering + line wrapping (§ Round-3 item 1) 193 **Group structure** (byte-verified over 160 409 records): the label is 194 `{grp}|{grp}|{grp}` where the groups are the *non-empty* ones among 195 `[premises, info, conclusions]`, in that order; the **info** group is always 196 present (100 %). An empty premise or conclusion group is dropped (a source rule 197 renders `{info}|{concl}`). 198 - **Info cell**: `#<temporal> : <RuleName>` and, if the rule has action facts, 199 `[<action>, …]` (e.g. `#i : I_Complete[Complete( … ), …]`, or bare `#vf.5 : Fresh`). 200 - **Premise/conclusion cell**: a single fact. 201 - **Fact spacing**: a fact pads — `Name( a, b )`, and `Name( )` with no args; 202 a **function application** does not — `f(a, b)`; a **tuple** is `<a, b>`. Verified 203 against the corpus. 204 - **Escaping**: inside a record label, `< > { } |` each get a leading backslash; 205 everything else (quotes, `~ $ ^ * ⊕`, spaces) is literal. 206 - **Line wrapping FORMAT** (byte-exact). When a group breaks, physical lines are 207 separated by `\l` (with a trailing `\l`), and each continuation line is indented 208 with a ` ` run **equal to the column of the broken group's first element** 209 (just after `( ` for a fact, after `<` for a tuple, after `[` for an action 210 list). Verified across 188 192 wrapped cells. 211 - **Line wrapping DECISION — RESOLVED (Session 4): a fixed-width paragraph fill, 212 width = 87 columns, measured per fact from column 0.** The Round-3 claim ("first 213 line 5..87, not a flat width, not derivable") is **refuted**: it *is* a flat 214 width. Established by driving crafted single-node theories 215 (`Out(<'a01', …, 'aN'>)`, `Out(<'aa…a', 'y'>)`) through the live server and 216 sweeping the term width one column at a time: 217 - **W = 87 absolute, functor-invariant.** A fact stays on one line iff its flat 218 rendering is ≤ 87 columns; at 88 it breaks. Tested with functor names of length 219 2, 3, 6, 10 (`Ba`,`Out`,`Fact12`,`Factabcdef`): the boundary was **always** at 220 flat width 87 fits / 88 breaks, so the budget is counted from the functor's own 221 column 0, *including* the functor name (a longer name simply fits fewer args). 222 Corpus-consistent: 99.2 % of 190 044 physical lines have fact-content ≤ 87; the 223 residual are single unbreakable atoms wider than 87 (which overflow verbatim). 224 - **Greedy paragraph fill.** When a group overflows, elements pack left-to-right; 225 the separator `, ` trails the element it follows and stays on the line, and the 226 next element wraps when it would pass column 87. Continuation lines use the 227 §3f indent. Example `Out(<'a01'…'a12'>)` (flat 91): line 0 = 228 `Out( <'a01', … 'a11', ` (eleven elements + trailing `, `, ending col 83), 229 `'a12'>` on the next line, then the fact's `)` on its own line. 230 - **Delimiter peel — BYTE-IMPLEMENTED (Session 5).** Re-probed the boundary with 231 fresh single-node sweeps (`E10..E14`, `W69..W74`, ports 3210): a **tuple's `>`** 232 stays with the last element iff it fits (`Out( <'aaa(72)', 'y'>` │ `)`), else it 233 peels onto its own line at the tuple's **`<` column** (`W74`: `…'y'` │ `<5sp>>` 234 │ `)`); a **fact's `)` ALWAYS** peels onto its own line at **col 0** once the 235 fact wraps — even when it would fit (`E12`: `'a12'>` │ `)`) — because the padded 236 ` )` space is the break. An **info action-list `]`** stays attached to its last 237 action. An unbreakable atom wider than the budget overflows verbatim; only the 238 trailing delimiters wrap. 239 - **Continuation lookahead — RESOLVED (subsumed).** The old "first line 11, 240 continuation 12" claim is a mis-read: the continuation packs **greedily to the 241 same width 87** as the first line (`E13`→2, `E14`→3 elements on the second line, 242 each ending well under 87). There is no ±1 combinator lookahead to reproduce. 243 - **Info action-list = vertical `sep`, not fill.** An overflowing info cell puts 244 **one action per line** (corpus `6738eb64…`: 4 actions → 4 lines, short actions 245 included), `]` on the last; whereas a tuple / fact-arg list uses the greedy 246 fill. Two distinct combinators, both byte-implemented. 247 - **`graph-clean::render::wrap_cell`** implements all of the above (`layout_fact` 248 fill+`)`-peel, `layout_tuple` fill+`>`-peel, `layout_info` vertical `sep`, 249 `run_layout` greedy fill, `split_top_commas`), wired into 250 `generate::build_record`; byte-verified against the 7 captured probe fixtures 251 (`wrap_E11..E14`, `wrap_W71/W72/W74`). 252 - **The wrap TRIGGER is a per-GROUP shared budget — RESOLVED (Session 6).** The 253 old per-cell `flat > 87` decision is wrong for multi-cell groups. Each record 254 group (`premises`, `info`, `conclusions`) is laid out **independently** (a 255 conclusion cell's wrap ignores premise/info widths, and vice versa — live 256 probes PM/R: a lone conclusion `Out` of flat 87 stays flat with premises of 257 flat 69/103/198 and rule names of length 40). Within a group, the cells share 258 a budget: 259 > For a premise/conclusion group, let `T = Σ(flat width of every cell in the 260 > group)`. Cell *i* wraps **iff** `flat_i > budget_i`, where 261 > `budget_i = max(87 − (T − flat_i), 20)`. Equivalently: **cell *i* wraps iff 262 > `T > 87` AND `flat_i > 20`.** 263 Established by controlled live 2-/3-cell sweeps (QUERIES §6): with a fixed 264 second cell of flat *q*, the first cell's fit/wrap boundary is exactly 265 `flat = 87 − q` (q=11 → 76/77, q=28 → 58/61, q=48 → 39/40, q=68 → 19/22); a 266 third cell adds into the same Σ (3-cell [p,28,28] flips at `flat = 87−56 = 31`). 267 The **floor 20** is a per-cell minimum budget (a cell of flat ≤ 20 never wraps; 268 live: Fb flat 98 forces the sibling budget negative yet the sibling fits at 269 flat ≤ 20, wraps at 21). Verified by hand on `ref_raw_1_1` (Wide rule): premise 270 `In` flat 67 does **not** wrap (budget 87−10 = 77) while conclusion `Big` flat 271 68 **does** (budget 87−(25+14) = 48); `Ack` flat 25 wraps (budget 20), `Out(h)` 272 flat 14 fits — all four correct. 273 - **The `#t : Rule[…]` INFO cell** is its own single-cell group (budget 87, 274 independent of prem/concl), with an added rule: **an info cell with ≥ 2 action 275 facts ALWAYS goes vertical** (one action per line), regardless of width — a 276 ≤ 1-action info wraps only when its flat > 87. Corpus census: 0 non-wrapped 277 info cells carry a top-level action-list comma (all ≥2-action infos wrap). 278 - **Corpus validation at scale.** The rule matches actual `\l` on **99.635 %** of 279 776 259 cells and **98.324 %** of 160 409 records (info cells 99.968 %). Every 280 one of the 2 831 residual cells is within ~1–2 columns of its budget (mismatch 281 `T`-distribution clusters entirely at 79 ≤ T ≤ 95). At that exact boundary the 282 outcome is a **±1 HughesPJ `fits`/ribbon rounding artifact** (live: at a group 283 total of exactly `budget+1` the fit/wrap flips with atom parity; at `budget+5` 284 it wraps cleanly), depending on the cell's token structure, order, and the port 285 marker widths — reproducing it byte-exactly needs the GPL pretty-printer's 286 internal `fits`. Two residuals remain **[GAP]**: (a) this ±1 boundary flip, and 287 (b) the exact greedy fill width when a *multi-cell* cell wraps (live probeB: the 288 per-line element count is not a clean function of the budget). The budget 289 **trigger** itself (does a cell wrap) is byte-exact away from the boundary. 290 291 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 292 ## 4. Clustering / simplification (§ priority 3) 293 294 **Trigger rule (byte-verified over all 14705 graphs, 0 violations):** 295 > The COMPACT clustered rendering (§2b, with `subgraph "cluster_…"` blocks) is 296 > used **iff the graph contains at least one node with `role` ≠ `"Undefined"`.** 297 > Otherwise the SIMPLE header (§2a) is used. 298 299 Non-Undefined roles arise from process/SAPIC agents (e.g. `Initiator`, 300 `Responder`, `server`, `Tag`, `v`). Pure multiset-rewriting theories emit the 301 simple header everywhere; role-annotated theories emit the compact header on 302 every non-empty graph (empty graphs have no role nodes ⇒ simple). 303 304 ### Cluster block 305 ``` 306 subgraph "cluster_<Role>_Session_<k>" { 307 nodesep="0.6"; 308 ranksep="0.6"; 309 label="<Role>_Session_<k>"; 310 style="filled"; 311 color="#RRGGBBAA"; 312 penwidth="2"; 313 fillcolor="#RRGGBBAA"; 314 overlap="false"; 315 sep="4"; 316 n<ID>[shape="record",label="…",fillcolor="#RRGGBB",style="filled",fontcolor="white",role="<Role>"]; 317 318 } 319 ``` 320 - Cluster name = `cluster_<clusterlabel>`; `label` = the clusterlabel 321 (`<Role>_Session_<k>`). Nodes grouped by (role, session instance). 322 - `color`/`fillcolor` are an 8-hex ARGB (alpha `4C`, ~30%) hashed from the 323 cluster; node `fillcolor` is the saturated per-role color (live SAPIC probe 324 `cluster_Process_Session_1` had node `fillcolor="#ffffff"`, `fontcolor="black"` 325 — so the node fill is not always saturated). Exact hash **[GAP]**. 326 327 ### Cluster EMISSION ORDER (§ Round-5 item 1) — byte-verified over 1457 corpus files 328 The top-level statement order of a clustered graph is **always** 329 `[free nodes] [clusters] [edges] [rankblock/legend] [invis edges]`: 0 files have a 330 top-level node after a cluster, 0 clusters after an edge. **Clusters contain only 331 records** (50 453 record cells, 0 ellipse/edge). A cluster's records occupy a 332 **contiguous** id range and clusters are ordered by increasing id (first-appearance 333 == id order). Since id order == emission order (§3e), the global id counter runs 334 over the free (non-role) nodes first — giving them the low ids — then over the 335 clustered records grouped by cluster. `graph-clean::generate` reproduces this: 336 Pass 1 allocates ids in `System.nodes` order, routing each role-record's statement 337 into its `cluster(label,color)` bucket and everything else to the free top level, 338 then emits free nodes, then cluster blocks in first-appearance order, then edges, 339 then the legend. Byte-exact on a live SAPIC single-cluster graph 340 (`cluster_process.dot`) and the multi-cluster corpus payload 341 `79c16911ad179d51.dot` (4 free ellipses + 1 cluster + 2 deduction edges). 342 343 ### What "simplification" means here 344 There is no exposed simplification *level*; the corpus reflects one fixed 345 rendering pipeline. The observable "simplification" is the fixed compression the 346 server applies before rendering: intruder-knowledge (`!KU`) nodes are drawn as 347 gray ellipses; single-premise/conclusion structural chains are drawn with bold 348 weight-10 edges; role nodes are packed into per-session clusters (compact mode). 349 Reproducing the *content* of that compression from a raw constraint system 350 requires the solver and is **[GAP]** — out of scope for a rendering crate. 351 352 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 353 ## 5. Node abbreviation (§ priority 2) 354 355 When a graph is drawn, sufficiently complex sub-terms are replaced by short 356 NAMES and listed in a legend node. Observed from 113 924 legend rows. 357 358 ### 5a. Legend node 359 A `plain` node with an HTML-like label, placed in a sink-rank block: 360 ``` 361 { 362 rank="sink"; 363 n<ID>[shape="plain",label=<<TABLE BORDER="1" CELLBORDER="0" CELLSPACING="3" CELLPADDING="1"><TR>…row0…</TR> 364 <65 spaces><TR>…row1…</TR> 365 … 366 <65 spaces><TR>…rowN…</TR></TABLE>>]; 367 368 } 369 ``` 370 - Each row: 371 `<TR><TD ALIGN="LEFT" VALIGN="TOP"><FONT COLOR="#000000">NAME</FONT></TD> <TD ALIGN="LEFT" VALIGN="TOP">=</TD> <TD ALIGN="LEFT" VALIGN="TOP">EXPANSION</TD></TR>` 372 (single spaces between `</TD>` and the next `<TD>`). 373 - The first `<TR>` is inline after the `<TABLE …>` tag; every continuation `<TR>` 374 is on its own line indented by **65 spaces = the byte length of the 375 `<TABLE BORDER="1" CELLBORDER="0" CELLSPACING="3" CELLPADDING="1">` opening 376 tag** (graphviz hang-indent of the table's children; independent of node id). 377 - EXPANSION is HTML-escaped: `<`→`<`, `>`→`>`, `&`→`&`. Single quotes 378 and `* ^ ~ $ ⊕` are literal. 379 - The legend node is wired to the rest with `style="invis"` edges (from a couple 380 of real nodes to the legend), emitted AFTER the sink block. 381 382 ### 5b. Naming scheme (byte-verified rule) 383 `NAME = <PREFIX><n>`, n = 1,2,3,… per prefix. 384 `PREFIX` = the first **two alphabetic characters, uppercased**, of the ROOT 385 symbol's *name* (one letter if the name has only one). The root symbol name is: 386 - function application `f(…)` ⇒ `f` (`sign`→SI, `senc`→SE, `hash`→HA, 387 `h`/`h1`/`h2`→H, `KDF`/`kdf1`→KD, `pk`→PK, `aenc`/`aead`→AE, `pmult`→PM, …) 388 - public constant `'c'` ⇒ `c` (`'uninitialized'`→UN, `'F_status'`→FS, 389 `'ho_req_ack'`→HO, `'no_message_state'`→NO — non-letters skipped: F,s→FS) 390 - variable `~v` / `$v` / `v` ⇒ `v` (`~AMF_UE_NGAP_ID`→AM, `~cid_N26`→CI, 391 `~gNB_UE_ID`→GN, `~mid…`→MI, `commitmsg`→CO, `StateChannel`→ST) 392 - infix operator, mapped to its function name then the letter rule: 393 `^`→exp→**EX**, `*`→mult→**MU**, `++`→union→**UN**, `⊕`→xor→**XO**. 394 395 Abbreviations NEST: an inner abbreviated term is referenced by name inside an 396 outer expansion (`KD1='KDF(EX1)'`, `MA1='mac(EX1, SI1)'`, `EX1="'g'^MU1"`, 397 `H1="h(<…, EX2, EX3, EX1, …>)"`). So naming is applied bottom-up. 398 399 Per-prefix numbering is by tamarin's canonical term order, NOT by first 400 appearance in the drawing (observed: `In(EX2)` is the first occurrence yet gets 401 `EX2`, and larger/deeper terms get the lower numbers within a prefix). The exact 402 tie-break total order is **[GAP]** (needs the solver's term ordering); the crate 403 assigns per-prefix numbers over a defined order and matches whenever that order 404 is unambiguous. 405 406 ### 5c. Selection — WHICH sub-terms get abbreviated **[RESOLVED — see REPORT2.md]** 407 408 **Rule (three necessary gates; each confirmed by a controlled black-box probe):** 409 > A sub-term `t` is abbreviated **iff** 410 > 1. `renderLen(t) ≥ 10` (Unicode-scalar length of the fully-expanded surface 411 > rendering, *including* `'…'` quotes and `~`/`$` sigils), AND 412 > 2. `occ(t) ≥ 2` (occurrences over the whole constraint system — graph node 413 > facts **and** the sequent's goals/formulas — edge-shared messages count 414 > once), AND 415 > 3. `t` is **not** a tuple `<…>`. 416 > Naming is bottom-up: nested eligible sub-terms are named first 417 > (`LO1='longarg123'`, then `H1='h(LO1)'`). 418 419 This is a rendered **length** gate, not the *size* or *occurrence* the earlier 420 note guessed at — which is why it looked context-free-yet-unexplained. It 421 cleanly settles the old counter-observations: `sign(<…,'g'^~ekR>,~ltkI)` is ≥10 422 chars (abbreviated); `KDF(z)` is 6 chars (never abbreviated, whatever its count); 423 `mac(z,SI1)` and `'g'^~lkR` are below the length/occurrence thresholds in their 424 graphs. `'g'^~lkR` vs `'g'^~lkR.1` differ because the `.1` copy reaches occ 2 in 425 the system while the other does not. 426 427 **Corpus-exactness (necessary direction):** across all **97 538** legend 428 expansions in **11 564** graphs, the minimum rendered length is exactly **10** 429 (0 below) and **0** entries are top-level tuples. Among terms drawn ≥2× in a 430 graph, **0** abbreviated terms violate len≥10 ∧ ¬tuple. Abbreviated terms with 431 graph-occurrence 1 (7 800) all reach ≥2 once the sequent is counted. 432 433 **Controlled probes (crafted theories, live server; see QUERIES.log):** 434 - `'12345678'` (10 chars, ×2) abbreviated; `'1234567'` (9, ×2) not ⇒ boundary = 10. 435 - `'ABCDEFGH'`/`'onceonly12'` (×1) not abbreviated; `'twicelong1'` (×2) is; 436 a 42-char constant (×1) not abbreviated ⇒ occ ≥ 2 is a hard gate at any length. 437 - `<'aa','bb','cc'>` (18 chars, ×2) not abbreviated while sibling `h('longarg123')` 438 (×2) is ⇒ tuple exclusion + bottom-up nesting. 439 440 **Residual (sufficiency):** for **AC/DH-operator** sub-terms (`^ * ⊕ ++ inv em 441 hp pmult`) tamarin counts occurrences over the *normalised* (AC-flattened, 442 DH-normal-form) term, not the surface rendering, so the same term can be 443 abbreviated in one proof state and not another (`em(hp($A),hp($B))` inline in 444 state `56eeb42d`, `EM1` in `54eb2e2c`). 93 % of apparent exceptions are such 445 AC/DH cases; the rest are validation artifacts from expanding shared 446 abbreviations before counting. Outside AC/DH terms the rule is exact. 447 448 The crate `abbrev::select` implements this rule (`MIN_ABBREV_LEN=10`, 449 `MIN_ABBREV_OCC=2`, tuple-excluded, bottom-up); tests reproduce every probe. 450 Byte-parity of the selection set is claimed for non-AC/DH terms and gated by the 451 caller supplying the full-system term multiset. 452 453 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 454 ## 6. System → graph mapping (§ Round-3 item 4) 455 456 From paired `main/proof` sequents and `interactive-graph-def` graphs, the 457 structural mapping a proof state yields (each point traces to §3d/§3f and the 458 content census; the *content* — which nodes/edges exist — is a solver GAP): 459 - a **rule instance** `#t : Rule[actions]` → a `record` node with groups 460 `{premises}|{info}|{conclusions}` (empty prem/concl dropped, info always kept); 461 - an **intruder-knowledge fact** → a gray `!KU( m ) @ #t` ellipse; 462 - a **protocol action/event** → a darkblue `Fact @ #t` ellipse; 463 - a **compressed intruder rule** → an uncolored `#t : rule` ellipse (§4 compression); 464 - an **unresolved node** referenced by an open premise → an `invtrapezium` 465 `(#var, idx)` (§3d); 466 - **edges** connect conclusion-port → premise-port (structural bold w10 [gray50]), 467 intruder deduction (red dashed), message (gray30), temporal order 468 (blue3/black dashed), etc. — the finite §3c vocabulary; 469 - ids from §3e; header from §4 (role); legend from §5. 470 471 `graph-clean::generate` implements this over an independent input model 472 (`System`/`GraphNode`/`RuleInstance`/`SysEdge`), taking node/edge lists, colors, 473 and cell text as INPUTS. `tests/generate_tests.rs` reproduces a live source-case 474 graph (`nsl_invtrap.dot`) **byte-exact** end-to-end. 475 476 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 477 ## 7. Simplification / abbreviation options (§ Round-3 item 5) 478 479 The UI's "Graph simplification off/L1/L2/L3" menu and abbreviation toggle are 480 cookie-driven and appended by the JS as graph-URL **query params** (mined from 481 `/static/js/tamarin-prover-ui.js`, confirmed by live diffs — QUERIES.log): 482 483 | param | UI sends when | observed effect | 484 |--------------------|----------------------------------|------------------------------------------| 485 | `simplification=N` | always (N=level, default 2) | **inert** — the *number* changes nothing (see below) | 486 | `uncompact=` | level 0 only | node COMPACTION off (lone intruder rule → full record vs ellipse) | 487 | `uncompress=` | level 0 only | system COMPRESSION off (Fresh sources + intruder rules re-expand to records) | 488 | `unabbreviate=` | abbreviate cookie off | abbreviation off (terms inlined, legend block omitted) | 489 | `no-auto-sources=` | auto-sources cookie off | disables auto-source precomputation | 490 | `clustering=true` | clustering cookie on | forces role clustering | 491 492 ### 7a. The L1/L2/L3 distinction — RESOLVED (channel-verified negative, Session 4) 493 494 The Round-3 GAP ("simplification 1/2/3 produced byte-identical graphs") is **not a 495 probing artifact and not a hidden session channel** — it is the genuine server 496 behavior: **there is no L1/L2/L3 distinction; the `simplification=N` *number* is 497 inert for every N.** Established under the session-state lens the follow-up 498 demanded: 499 500 - **Channel.** The served JS (`tamarin-prover-ui.js`, lines 78–100 / 476–500) 501 reads a *client-side* `simplification` cookie (default 2) and converts it into 502 the graph-URL query param; at level 0 it *additionally* appends `uncompact=` + 503 `uncompress=`. Levels 1/2/3 send **only** `simplification=N`. The server emits 504 **no `Set-Cookie` on any response** (root, case-graph, main/cases json, proof 505 method) — there is *no server-side session state*; the param is the only channel. 506 - **Change verified before diffing.** Driving the *flags* through the URL param 507 demonstrably changes the graph (e.g. rich NSLPK3 case `refined/6/5`: default 508 5 668 B → `uncompact=&uncompress=` 7 792 B), and the result is byte-identical 509 whether or not a matching cookie is also sent. So the URL param **is** honored. 510 - **The number is inert.** On that 17-node case graph and on a **39-node, 27 KB** 511 DH/bilinear proof-state graph (`eurosp19-eccDAA` analysed, 512 `proof/functional_correctness/_`), every probe of `simplification` ∈ 513 {0,1,2,3,4,5,10,99} — via URL param, via cookie, and via both together, and 514 crossed with every flag combination — collapses to a **single** md5. `flags_L0` 515 … `flags_L99` are byte-identical. (Second theory NAXOS_eCK case `refined/4/1` 516 reproduces this.) 517 - **Mechanism.** The UI's four-item menu (`for i<4`: off / L1 / L2 / L3) dresses up 518 a *binary*: "off" (level 0) = the UI adds the two flags; "L1/L2/L3" = no flags, 519 and the server never consumes the level integer. The real transforms are the 520 independent boolean flags, each with a distinct, measured effect on the 39-node 521 graph: `uncompact=` 39 nodes→richer records (27 361→30 880 B); `uncompress=` 522 39→**74** nodes (re-expands compressed chains, 31 407 B); `unabbreviate=` drops 523 the legend node (39→38) and inlines terms (36 690 B, 0 legend rows); 524 level 0 (`uncompact`+`uncompress`) = 74 nodes, byte-identical to the two flags 525 sent explicitly. 526 527 `graph-clean::options::Options` models the knobs and reproduces the exact query 528 string; `simplification=N` is carried but is documented inert. The abbreviation 529 on/off transform is applied directly (drop legend / inline). Remaining **GAP** 530 here: the *content* of compress/compact (a solver transform — the caller supplies 531 the already-(un)compressed node set). 532 533 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 534 ## 8. What the crate reproduces vs. gaps 535 536 Reproduced & byte-tested against captured/live payloads: 537 - DOT document assembly: both headers, block/whitespace rule, node lines 538 (record/ellipse/plain/**invtrapezium**), edge lines with ports & attrs, cluster 539 subgraphs, legend sink-block + invis edges, empty graph. Round-trip reproduces 540 **12 022/12 022** corpus payloads byte-exact (`tests/roundtrip.rs`). 541 - **Node-id/port allocation** (§3e): `NodeIdAllocator`; validated 12 022/12 022 via 542 the crate model (`tests/alloc_corpus.rs`). 543 - **Record-cell rendering** (§3f): fact/function/tuple spacing, info-cell shape, 544 group-drop rule, record escaping, and the wrap **DECISION + FORMAT + PEEL** — 545 `wrap_cell` (`FILL_WIDTH=87`, greedy `fillSep` for fact-arg/tuple lists, vertical 546 `sep` for info action lists, tuple-`>` peel to the `<` column, fact-`)` peel to 547 col 0), wired into generation and byte-verified against 7 live boundary probes. 548 - **System → graph GENERATION** (§4, §6): `generate` over the independent `System` 549 model — free/ellipse/record/invtrapezium nodes, **role clusters** (`subgraph 550 cluster_…`, free-then-cluster id/emission order), the **`#last`/bare-timepoint** 551 ellipse, the finite edge vocabulary, id/port allocation, and the wired record-cell 552 wrap. Byte-exact on live + corpus fixtures (invtrap, single/multi cluster, 553 `#last`, wrap sweep) in `tests/generate_tests.rs`. 554 - **Pre-rendered-cell interop** (§ round 5): `RawRule` / `GraphNode::RawRule` accept 555 already-rendered cell strings and run them through the same wrap/escape pipeline, 556 byte-identical to the Term-based path (`raw_rule_matches_term_path_byte_exact`). 557 - **Simplification/abbreviation options** (§7): `Options` query-string model; the 558 **L1/L2/L3 level number is proven inert** (§7a) — channel-verified negative. 559 - Abbreviation naming, numbering, legend HTML (65-space indent), and the SELECTION 560 rule (§5c, REPORT2.md), plus the cluster/compact trigger (§4). 561 562 Documented gaps (need the GPL solver or an unavailable backend): 563 - JSON graph backend format (unavailable / not in corpus). 564 - **Record-cell wrap TRIGGER** (§3f): the per-GROUP shared-budget rule 565 (`budget_i = max(87 − Σ(other cell flats in the group), 20)`, wrap iff 566 `flat_i > budget_i`; info-cell ≥2-actions-always-vertical) is byte-implemented in 567 `render::wrap_cell` + `generate::build_record` and matches 98.3 % of records / 568 99.6 % of cells at corpus scale. Two boundary residuals remain **[GAP]**: (a) the 569 ±1 HughesPJ `fits` rounding flip when a cell sits exactly at its budget, and (b) 570 the exact greedy fill width when a *multi-cell* cell wraps — both need the GPL 571 pretty-printer's internal `fits` and are documented in §3f. 572 - **compress/compact content** (§4, §6): which nodes/edges a raw constraint system 573 yields — a solver transform. (The L1/L2/L3 level distinction is no longer a gap: 574 it is proven non-existent, §7a.) 575 - Per-rule/per-cluster **color hashes** (§3a, §4); the `trapezium` dual (§3d, unobserved). 576 - Abbreviation of AC/DH sub-terms (§5c residual, normalised-form occurrence). 577 - Canonical per-prefix numbering tie-break (§5b); empty-prefix start index. 578

1	# REPORT2 — the abbreviation SELECTION rule (BEHAVIOR.md §5c gap)
2	
3	Clean-room continuation of `graphdot`. Everything below traces to observed
4	oracle output: the captured DOT corpus (`oracle/dot_corpus/`, 12 022 unique
5	payloads), the captured crawl manifests (`oracle/captured_responses/`, 81
6	theories; the `main/proof` json giving the full sequent paired 1:1 with each
7	`interactive-graph-def` DOT), and controlled probes of the black-box server
8	(`hs_server` binary, `--port=` form). No tamarin-prover source was read.
9	
10	## Result in one line
11	
12	> A sub-term `t` of a constraint system is abbreviated in the graph **iff**
13	> (1) its rendered length >= **10**, (2) it occurs >= **2** times in the system,
14	> and (3) it is not a tuple `<...>`. Naming is bottom-up (nested eligible
15	> sub-terms are named first).
16	
17	The prior named puzzle -- why `sign(...)` at 3 occurrences is abbreviated while
18	`KDF(z)` at 4 is not -- is resolved: the gate is rendered **string length**, not
19	structural size or occurrence count. `sign(<...>,...)` is >=10 chars; `KDF(z)`
20	is 6. The earlier investigator measured *size* and *occurrence* and so could not
21	separate them.
22	
23	## Evidence
24	
25	### (1) Length >= 10 -- necessary, corpus-exact
26	Over **all 97 538** legend expansions in **11 564** graphs (every legend in the
27	corpus), the minimum fully-expanded rendered length is **exactly 10**; **zero**
28	are shorter. Histogram floor: len10=7397, len11=3071, len12=3776, ... nothing at
29	5-9. Length is measured on tamarin's own rendering *including* surface
30	decorations -- quotes, `~`/`$` sigils (a bare `pk(PL1)` is only 7 chars but is
31	abbreviated because it *expands* to >=10; the decision is on the full expansion).
32	
33	Controlled probe (`LenTest.spthy`): a rule whose one node emits
34	`Keep(~x, '12345678', '12345678', '1234567', '1234567')`. Observed graph:
35	`Keep( ~x, 2, 2, '1234567', '1234567' )` with legend `2 = '12345678'`.
36	`'12345678'` = 10 chars -> abbreviated; `'1234567'` = 9 chars, **same occurrence
37	count** -> not. The boundary is exactly 10.
38	
39	### (2) Occurrence >= 2 -- necessary, hard gate independent of length
40	Structural per-graph analysis of 5 725 graphs (156 602 distinct candidate
41	sub-terms): among terms drawn >=2 times, **every** abbreviated term satisfies
42	len>=10 AND not-tuple -- **0** counterexamples. The 7 800 abbreviated terms the
43	graph-only occurrence "misses" all have graph-occurrence 1: they occur once in
44	the *drawn* graph but >=2 in the full system (the sequent's goals/formulas). This
45	is why terms such as `SI3 = sign(~y, k)` appear in a legend while being drawn
46	**nowhere** in the graph -- the abbreviation universe is the whole constraint
47	system, not the drawn nodes. Pairing each DOT with its `main/proof` json sequent
48	recovers those second occurrences.
49	
50	Controlled probe (`OccTest.spthy`): `'ABCDEFGH'` (10 chars, x1) and
51	`'onceonly12'` (12 chars, x1) are **not** abbreviated; `'twicelong1'` (12, x2)
52	**is** (`TW1`). Probe (`LongOnce.spthy`): a **42-char** constant appearing once
53	is **not** abbreviated, while a 12-char constant appearing twice is. So there is
54	no "very long => abbreviate at 1 occurrence" branch -- occ >= 2 is a hard gate.
55	
56	### (3) Tuples are never abbreviated -- necessary, corpus-exact
57	**0 of 97 538** legend expansions is a top-level tuple. Only function
58	applications, exponentiations, AC-operator terms (`* (+) ++`), and atoms (long
59	constants/variables) are abbreviated. Controlled probe (`TupTest.spthy`):
60	`<'aa','bb','cc'>` (18 chars, x2) stays inline, while the sibling
61	`h('longarg123')` (x2) is abbreviated to `H1` with `LO1 = 'longarg123'`,
62	`H1 = h(LO1)` -- confirming the tuple exclusion and **bottom-up nesting** (inner
63	`'longarg123'` named before the enclosing `h(...)`), and the prefix rule
64	(`lo`->LO, `h`->H).
65	
66	## Accuracy and the residual
67	
68	The three gates are **necessary** with 0 corpus counterexamples (length, tuple)
69	/ 0 counterexamples among drawn->=2 terms (occurrence). For **sufficiency** --
70	is every (len>=10 AND occ>=2 AND not-tuple) term abbreviated? -- the controlled
71	probes say yes for ordinary terms, and the corpus agrees except for one
72	characterized residual:
73	
74	* **93.3%** of apparent sufficiency-exceptions occur in graphs using an **AC/DH
75	  operator** (`^ * (+) ++ inv em hp pmult`). Example: in state `56eeb42d`,
76	  `em(hp($A), hp($B))` (18 chars, drawn 7x) is *not* abbreviated, yet in a later
77	  state `54eb2e2c` the *same* term *is* (`EM1`). The difference is how DH
78	  exponentiation groups the term: tamarin counts occurrences over the
79	  **normalised** (AC-flattened, DH-normal-form) term, which a surface count does
80	  not model. This is solver-normalisation-dependent and cannot be derived from
81	  the rendering alone.
82	
83	* The remaining **~7%** are terms nested inside a *shared* abbreviated term
84	  (e.g. `pk(~ska.3)` inside one `sign(...)` threaded through an edge and drawn as
85	  `SI` in two facts). These are **measurement artifacts** of validating by
86	  *expanding* abbreviations before counting: expansion turns one edge-shared
87	  occurrence into several. The occurrence gate counts occurrences in the actual
88	  constraint system (an edge-shared message counts once), so these are not true
89	  rule violations -- a direct search finds **no** graph where such a term appears
90	  literally >=2x and is left un-abbreviated.
91	
92	Net: outside AC/DH-normalised sub-terms the rule is exact (confirmed by
93	controlled probe and by the AC-free portion of the corpus); the one honest gap
94	is the AC/DH occurrence-over-normal-form count.
95	
96	## What was implemented (`graph-clean`)
97	
98	`abbrev::select(roots) -> Vec<Term>` implements the rule: enumerate every
99	non-tuple sub-term of `roots` with `render_len() >= 10`, keep those occurring
100	>= 2 times, return them shortest-first (bottom-up). Thresholds are exposed as
101	`MIN_ABBREV_LEN=10`, `MIN_ABBREV_OCC=2` and via `select_with(roots,min_len,
102	min_occ)` for probing. `roots` must be the full multiset of constraint-system
103	terms (graph node-fact arguments **and** sequent terms) because occurrence is a
104	whole-system count. Feeding the result through the existing `Abbreviator`
105	reproduces the legend end-to-end.
106	
107	`term::Term` gained `is_tuple()` and `render_len()` (Unicode-scalar count of the
108	full rendering, matching the observed 10-char boundary).
109	
110	Tests (`tests/abbrev_tests.rs`, all green) reproduce each controlled probe:
111	`select_length_boundary_is_ten`,
112	`select_requires_two_occurrences_even_for_long_terms`,
113	`select_never_abbreviates_a_tuple`, `select_then_name_reproduces_probe_legend`,
114	`select_counts_nested_occurrences`, `select_with_custom_thresholds`. Full suite
115	is 24 tests incl. the 12 022-payload byte-exact roundtrip.
116	
117	## Minor open detail
118	A numeric-only constant (`'12345678'`, no alphabetic chars) gets an **empty
119	prefix**, so its abbreviation name is a bare number -- observed as `2` for the
120	first such entry. The starting index for empty-prefix names was not pinned down
121	(single observation); it does not affect the length/occurrence/tuple rule.
122	
123	================================================================================
124	
125	# REPORT — Round 6: the record-cell wrap TRIGGER (BEHAVIOR.md §3f gap)
126	
127	Clean-room continuation of `graphdot`. Everything below traces to observed oracle
128	output: the captured DOT corpus (`oracle/dot_corpus/`, 12 022 payloads / 160 409
129	records) and controlled probes of the black-box interactive server (autoprove →
130	`interactive-graph-def`). No tamarin-prover source was read.
131	
132	## Result in one line
133	
134	> The old per-cell rule ("a cell wraps iff its own flat width > 87") is wrong for
135	> multi-cell record groups. Each record group (premises, info, conclusions) is laid
136	> out **independently**, and within a group the cells **share** the fill width:
137	> a cell wraps **iff** `group_total_flat > 87` **AND** `cell_flat > 20`
138	> — equivalently `cell_flat > max(87 − Σ(other cell flats in the group), 20)`.
139	> The `#t : Rule[…]` info cell is its own single-cell group (budget 87) with an
140	> extra rule: an info list of **≥ 2 action facts always goes vertical**, whatever
141	> its width.
142	
143	The prior puzzle — first-line widths spanning 19..277 with "no fixed page width" —
144	is resolved: 87 is a *per-group* budget shared across cells, so a cell deep in a
145	wide group wraps far below 87 while a lone cell tolerates 87. The In-vs-Big pair in
146	`ref_raw_1_1.dot` is the crisp witness: `In(<x1.4..x10.4>)` flat 67 (premise, alone
147	with Fr) does NOT wrap, while the byte-identical `Big(<x1.4..x10.4>)` flat 68
148	(conclusion, sharing with Ack+Out) DOES.
149	
150	## Evidence
151	
152	### (1) Groups are independent — controlled probes
153	- Growing premises (In flat 69/103/198) or the rule name (len 40) leaves a lone
154	  conclusion `Out` (flat 87) flat; adding ONE preceding conclusion (flat 12) makes
155	  it wrap. ⇒ a conclusion's budget counts only other conclusions.
156	
157	### (2) Shared budget = 87 − Σ(others), floor 20 — step-1 sweeps
158	- 2-cell group [Fa(p), Fb(q)]: with q fixed, Fa's fit/wrap boundary is exactly
159	  `flat = 87 − q_flat` (q=11 → 76/77, q=28 → 58/61, q=48 → 39/40, q=68 → 19/22).
160	- 3-cell [p,28,28] flips at `flat = 87 − 56 = 31` ⇒ Σ is over all other cells.
161	- Floor: a sibling forced far past budget still leaves the target fitting at flat
162	  ≤ 20, wrapping at 21 ⇒ per-cell minimum budget 20.
163	- Algebra: `flat > max(87−Σothers, 20)` ⟺ `(Σall > 87) ∧ (flat > 20)`.
164	
165	### (3) Info cell — separate probes
166	- A single-action info wraps by width at flat > 87 (independent of prem/concl).
167	- An info with ≥ 2 actions ALWAYS wraps vertically (TwoShort flat 34, AB flat 22).
168	  Corpus census: **0** non-wrapped info cells carry a top-level action-list comma.
169	
170	### (4) Corpus-exactness
171	- The rule matches actual `\l` on **99.635 %** of 776 259 cells and **98.324 %** of
172	  160 409 records (info cells 99.968 %). Hand-verified on the Wide record: In (77,
173	  fits), Big (budget 48, wraps), Ack (budget 20, wraps), Out(h) (14, fits) — all
174	  four correct; and on the 2-action info cells of `b5a8773`/`001113` (both wrap).
175	
176	## Accuracy and the residual (honest gaps)
177	
178	Every one of the 2 831 residual cells sits within ~1–2 columns of its budget
179	(mismatch group-totals cluster entirely at 79 ≤ T ≤ 95). At that exact boundary the
180	outcome is a **±1 rounding artifact of tamarin's HughesPJ `fits`**: a live split
181	sweep at group-total = `budget + 1` flips fit/wrap with atom parity, while at
182	`budget + 5` it wraps cleanly. A second residual: the exact greedy fill **width**
183	when a *multi-cell* cell wraps is not a clean function of the budget (probeB: a W
184	tuple keeps a 9-element first line for two different Pad widths / budgets), so the
185	per-line element count of a multi-cell wrap is not byte-exact. Both need the GPL
186	pretty-printer's internal `fits`; the **trigger** (does a cell wrap) is byte-exact
187	away from the ±1 boundary.
188	
189	## What was implemented (`graph-clean`)
190	
191	- `render::cell_budget(flats, i) = max(87 − Σ_{j≠i} flats, 20)` and
192	  `render::count_info_actions`; `MIN_CELL_BUDGET = 20`.
193	- `render::wrap_cell_budget(flat, budget)` threads the group budget through the
194	  fill/peel machinery (the old `wrap_cell` = `wrap_cell_budget(flat, 87)`, so all
195	  single-cell fixtures are unchanged). Info cells force the vertical action `sep`
196	  when ≥ 2 actions. A **fact-argument** break now ends the line at the bare `,`
197	  (byte-exact vs `Init_1(...)`/Ack), while a **tuple-element** break keeps the `, `.
198	- `generate::build_record` computes per-group budgets (premises together,
199	  conclusions together, info alone) and wraps each cell with its budget.
200	- Tests: `cell_budget_shares_the_group_width`, `group_trigger_matches_wide_record`,
201	  `multi_arg_fact_break_drops_the_comma_space`, `count_info_actions_counts_top_level`,
202	  `info_two_actions_always_vertical_even_when_short`. Full suite green; the
203	  GRAPHCLEAN_CORPUS round-trip stays **12 022/12 022** byte-exact.
204	
205	## Opportunistic: role → color (characterized, not implemented)
206	
207	Corpus mining (15 non-`Undefined` roles): the record `fillcolor` is a **deterministic
208	function of the role name** (role `A` → `#804046` in all 3321 instances, `B` →
209	`#628040`, `Tag` → `#40807c`, `Process` → `#ffffff`, …), and the cluster ARGB is a
210	deterministic function of the role too (`A_Session_k` → `#D836744C` for every k,
211	`B` → `#3671D84C`, `Initiator` → `#4936D84C`) — the session index does not affect
212	color. So the color IS name-determined (a necessary condition for derivability), and
213	the values look like a name-hash → hue → HSV rendering; but the node hue and cluster
214	hue differ per role (two distinct derivations), so the exact hash is unresolved.
215	Reverse-engineering it needs a dedicated live campaign of crafted novel role names
216	(like §5b's per-prefix numbering) — left as a documented open item; the crate keeps
217	colors as caller inputs.

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Round-7 probe theory generator + DOT cell parser (clean-room, black-box).

We build one-rule theories whose single graph node is a record with a
controlled conclusion (or premise) group, autoprove an exists-trace lemma per
rule, fetch the proof-state graph DOT, and read back the wrapped layout of a
target cell.  All widths are engineered from distinct <10-char / occ-1 atoms so
node abbreviation (len>=10 & occ>=2) never fires and the flat widths are exact.
"""
import re, sys

# ---- fact builders (flat widths are exact & abbreviation-proof) --------------

def tup(n, start=1):
    """A tuple fact body of n distinct 5-char public constants 'eNN'."""
    return "<" + ", ".join(f"'e{start+i:02d}'" for i in range(n)) + ">"

def big(n, name="Big", start=1):
    """Wrapping-tuple cell: NAME(<'e01',...,'eNN'>). flat width computed by measure."""
    return f"{name}({tup(n, start)})"

def atom_fact(flat, name):
    """A single-atom fact NAME('bbb..') whose flat rendering is exactly `flat`.
    flat = len(NAME)+2 (for '( ') + (L+2) quotes/atom + 2 (' )'); L = atom chars."""
    L = flat - (len(name) + 2) - 2 - 2
    if L < 1:
        raise ValueError(f"flat {flat} too small for name {name}")
    return f"{name}('{'b'*L}')"

def flat_of(term_str):
    """Flat visual width of a rendered fact string == its char count."""
    return len(term_str)

# ---- theory assembly ---------------------------------------------------------

def build_theory(name, rules):
    """rules: list of (lemma_name, [conclusion fact strings]).
    Each becomes  rule R: [Fr(~s)] --[R()]-> [concls]  + exists-trace lemma."""
    out = [f"theory {name}", "begin", ""]
    lem = []
    for rn, concls in rules:
        body = ", ".join(concls)
        out.append(f"rule {rn}:\n  [ Fr(~s) ] --[ {rn}() ]-> [ {body} ]\n")
        lem.append(f'lemma l_{rn}: exists-trace "Ex #i. {rn}() @ #i"')
    out += lem + ["", "end", ""]
    return "\n".join(out)

# ---- DOT record-cell parser --------------------------------------------------

def find_record_label(dot):
    m = re.search(r'shape="record",label="(.*?)",fillcolor=', dot)
    return m.group(1) if m else None

def split_groups(label):
    """label = {{g0}|{g1}|{g2}} -> list of group inner strings (cells joined by |)."""
    assert label.startswith("{{") and label.endswith("}}"), label[:40]
    inner = label[2:-2]
    # split on }|{ at top level
    return re.split(r'\}\|\{', inner)

def split_cells(group):
    """group inner = <p0> cell0|<p1> cell1|... -> list of raw cell texts (port stripped)."""
    # cells separated by | that are NOT escaped (\|) and not inside the cell text.
    # Ports look like <nK> ; split on '|<n' but keep structure.
    parts = re.split(r'\|(?=<n\d+> )', group)
    cells = []
    for p in parts:
        m = re.match(r'<n\d+> (.*)$', p, re.S)
        cells.append(m.group(1) if m else p)
    return cells

def unescape_cell(cell):
    """Undo record escaping for width/element analysis: \< \> \{ \} \| -> literal."""
    return re.sub(r'\\([<>{}|])', r'\1', cell)

def physical_lines(cell):
    """Split a wrapped cell into physical lines. Segments end with \\l; continuation
    lines are prefixed by   runs. Returns list of (indent, text) unescaped."""
    if '\\l' not in cell:
        return [(0, unescape_cell(cell))]
    segs = cell.split('\\l')
    if segs and segs[-1] == '':
        segs = segs[:-1]
    out = []
    for s in segs:
        m = re.match(r'((?: )*)(.*)$', s, re.S)
        ind = len(m.group(1)) // len(' ')
        out.append((ind, unescape_cell(m.group(2))))
    return out

def line0_tuple_elements(cell):
    """For a wrapping tuple-fact cell like NAME( <'e01', 'e02', ... >, count the
    number of tuple elements on physical line 0."""
    lines = physical_lines(cell)
    l0 = lines[0][1]
    # strip leading NAME( < ; count 'eNN' tokens on the line
    return len(re.findall(r"'e\d+'", l0)), l0, lines

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Batch A: pin the effective FILL budget of a wrapping tuple cell as a function
of one sibling's flat width, in both group orders, incl. wrapping siblings.

Target cell = Tgt(<'PAD'(W), 'y'>): sweep W; 'y' stays on line 0 iff
budget >= W+11, peels otherwise -> budget = (max W with 'y' on line0) + 11.
Sibling = a single-atom fact of an exact flat width q (never a tuple)."""
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, '/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/graphdot/workspace/r7')
from genlib import build_theory, atom_fact

def tgt(w):
    # Tgt( <'aaa..(W)', 'y'> ) ; flat = W+14
    return f"Tgt(<'{'a'*w}', 'y'>)"

rules = []
meta = []

def add(rn, concls, note):
    rules.append((rn, concls)); meta.append((rn, note))

# Sibling flat widths to test (single-atom fact Sib('bb..') ; min flat 10).
# small/non-wrapping (<=20), medium, and large/wrapping (>20).
SIBS = [10, 12, 14, 16, 18, 20, 24, 28, 34, 40, 48, 58, 68, 78, 88, 98]
# For each sibling, sweep W around the expected break.  Expected budget ~ 87-q,
# so 'y' peel near W ~ (87-q)-11 = 76-q ; sweep W in [max(20,66-q) .. 82-q].
for q in SIBS:
    center = 76 - q
    lo = max(18, center - 8); hi = center + 8
    sib = atom_fact(q, "Sib")
    for w in range(lo, hi + 1):
        # order 1: target first
        add(f"AF_q{q}_w{w}", [tgt(w), sib], f"2cell TGT,SIB q={q} W={w} tgtflat={w+14}")
        # order 2: sibling first
        add(f"AS_q{q}_w{w}", [sib, tgt(w)], f"2cell SIB,TGT q={q} W={w} tgtflat={w+14}")

# Exact reproduction of the Wide datum: 3-cell [big68-ish, 25, 14] in given order,
# using a countable tuple for the 68 cell so we read its line-0 element count.
# Big = Tgt2(<10 x 5-char elems>) ~ flat 6+ ... measured live; sibs 25 & 14.
def bigN(n):
    return "Big(" + "<" + ", ".join(f"'e{i:02d}'" for i in range(1, n+1)) + ">)"
add("WIDE_big25_14", [bigN(10), atom_fact(25, "Ack"), atom_fact(14, "Out")], "wide-repro [big,25,14]")
add("WIDE_25_big_14", [atom_fact(25, "Ack"), bigN(10), atom_fact(14, "Out")], "wide-repro [25,big,14]")
add("WIDE_14_25_big", [atom_fact(14, "Out"), atom_fact(25, "Ack"), bigN(10)], "wide-repro [14,25,big]")

thy = build_theory("R7A", rules)
open(sys.argv[1], "w").write(thy)
open(sys.argv[1] + ".meta", "w").write("\n".join(f"{n}\t{d}" for n, d in meta))
print("rules:", len(rules))

    for g in split_groups(label):

    tag = name[2:-4]

    wm = min(wr) if wr else None
    sig = others_sum(key)

def atom_fact(flat, name, fill='b'):
    """A single-atom fact NAME('bbb..') whose flat rendering is exactly `flat`.
    flat = len(NAME)+2 (for '( ') + (L+2) quotes/atom + 2 (' )'); L = atom chars.
    `fill` MUST differ between sibling facts of equal length, else the >=10-char

    L = flat - (len(name) + 2) - 2 - 2
    if L < 1:

    return f"{name}('{fill*L}')"

"""Test conservative occ-relief refinements of the flat-sum trigger against the
corpus (using ACTUAL occ from the rendering as the best case).
M0: flatsum.

    smaller than every sibling that wraps (the disparate small-cell case).
Mb: like Ma but also require i to be the unique global min-flat cell in the group.

import re, glob
CORP="/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/graphdot/oracle/dot_corpus"
def split_top(s, sep='|'):
    parts=[]; depth=0; cur=[]; i=0
    while i<len(s):
        c=s[i]
        if c=='\\' and i+1<len(s): cur.append(s[i:i+2]); i+=2; continue
        if c=='{': depth+=1; cur.append(c)
        elif c=='}': depth-=1; cur.append(c)
        elif c==sep and depth==0: parts.append(''.join(cur)); cur=[]
        else: cur.append(c)
        i+=1
    parts.append(''.join(cur)); return parts
def unescape(t):
    for a,b in [('\\<','<'),('\\>','>'),('\\{','{'),('\\}','}'),('\\|','|')]: t=t.replace(a,b)
    return t
def phys(cell):
    if '\\l' not in cell: return [len(unescape(cell))]
    segs=cell.split('\\l');
    if segs and segs[-1]=='': segs=segs[:-1]
    o=[]
    for s in segs:
        m=re.match(r'((?: )*)(.*)$', s, re.S); o.append(len(m.group(1))//6+len(unescape(m.group(2))))
    return o
def flat_width(cell):
    lost=len(re.findall(r',\\l',cell))+len(re.findall(r'\\l\)',cell))
    return len(unescape(cell.replace('\\l','').replace(' ',''))) + lost
def occ(cell): return max(phys(cell))
def parse_record(label):
    if not (label.startswith('{') and label.endswith('}')): return None
    o=[]
    for g in split_top(label[1:-1]):
        g=g.strip()
        if not (g.startswith('{') and g.endswith('}')): return None
        cs=[]
        for c in split_top(g[1:-1]):
            m=re.match(r'<n\d+> (.*)$', c, re.S)
            if not m: return None
            cs.append(m.group(1))
        o.append(cs)
    return o
def is_info(cells): return len(cells)==1 and re.match(r'#\S+ : ', cells[0])

tot=0; m0=0; ma=0; mb=0
for f in sorted(glob.glob(CORP+"/*.dot")):
    for line in open(f):
        if 'shape="record"' not in line: continue
        m=re.search(r'label="(.*)",fillcolor', line)
        if not m: continue
        groups=parse_record(m.group(1))
        if groups is None: continue
        for cells in groups:

            wraps=['\\l' in c for c in cells]

                smaller_than_wrapsibs = wrapping_sibs and all(fw < s for s in wrapping_sibs)

print(f"Ma occ-relief guard: {ma} ({100*ma/tot:.4f}%)")
print(f"Mb +unique-min     : {mb} ({100*mb/tot:.4f}%)")

def occ_budgets(flat_cells, flats):
    n=len(flats)
    order=sorted(range(n), key=lambda i: flats[i])
    occ=list(flats); fill=[0]*n
    for i in order:
        others=sum(occ[j] for j in range(n) if j!=i)
        b=max(87-others,20); fill[i]=b
        occ[i]=cell_occ(flat_cells[i], b)
    return fill

def minbud_budgets(flats):
    # each sibling occupies min(flat, its flat-sum budget): a wrapping sibling
    # fills its allocated budget, a fitting one its flat.
    n=len(flats)

def minbud_budgets(flats):
    # each sibling occupies min(flat, its flat-sum budget): a wrapping sibling
    # fills its allocated budget, a fitting one its flat.
    n=len(flats)
    contrib=[min(flats[j], cell_budget(flats,j)) for j in range(n)]

def greedy_budgets(flats):
    # smallest-flat-first; a processed sibling occupies min(flat, its assigned
    # budget), an un-processed (wider) one its full flat.
    n=len(flats)
    order=sorted(range(n), key=lambda i: flats[i])
    contrib=list(flats); budget=[0]*n
    for i in order:
        others=sum(contrib[j] for j in range(n) if j!=i)
        b=max(87-others,20); budget[i]=b
        contrib[i]=min(flats[i], b)
    return budget
